task.title: Add comment-only receipt proving no behavior changes occurred in learning-comments mode.

context.problem_statement: Learning-comments mode is intentionally behavior-neutral, but the prompt and receipt contract do not yet require structured proof that a run changed only comments or explanation artifacts.
background: AGW-078 kept formal receipt/schema enforcement separate from non-developer explainability.
schemas/session-receipt.schema.json already records run.mode, scope.behavior_change, commands, tests, docs impact, and disposition.
scripts/verify_agent_receipt.py is the local strict receipt gate.
non_goals: Do not implement stale-comment scanning from AGW-031 or ADR fallback behavior from AGW-045.
Do not add repo-contract-kit installer behavior in this packet.
Do not change runtime behavior outside prompt/schema/validator/docs/tests.

expected_outputs: Learning-comments prompt requires a named comment-only receipt section.
Receipt schema can represent comment-only verification evidence.
Strict receipt validation fails learning-comments receipts that omit comment-only proof or claim behavior changes.
Tests cover passing and failing learning-comments receipt cases.

acceptance_criteria.description: The canonical learning-comments prompt requires a structured comment-only receipt with diff scope, behavior-change assertion, evidence commands, and explicit uncertainty handling.
The session receipt schema includes machine-readable comment-only verification evidence without breaking existing receipt fields.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Read schemas/session-receipt.schema.json and run focused receipt tests.

acceptance_criteria.description: Strict receipt validation enforces learning-comments invariants: scope.behavior_change must be false, comment-only evidence must be present, non-comment paths or uncertainty must be explicit, and missing proof fails.
The Codex adapter mirrors the canonical prompt.
Documentation tells operators where the receipt evidence belongs.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Read changed docs and run docs checks.

readiness_check.command: make agent-task-ready TASK=AGW-044 TASK_READY_JSON=1 or record why unavailable for this non-worktree parent-run packet
readiness_check.expected_result: ready report passes or blocker is recorded

lifecycle_action.command: make agent-task-finalize TASK=AGW-044 TASK_RECEIPT=<path> TASK_FINALIZE_JSON=1 or lifecycle fallback
lifecycle_action.expected_result: metadata is closed and final receipt is linked, or fallback receipt is durable

final_task_status.expected_result: task is terminal or no local metadata was created and the reason is recorded

closeout_preview.expected_result: eligible, retained, or blocked cleanup state is recorded

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the checkout is clean, only expected files are dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This changes a user-facing prompt workflow and receipt contract.

known_risks: Overly strict validation could reject legitimate no-edit explanation-note runs.
The schema and Python validator can drift if tests cover only one surface.
Prompt wording must avoid encouraging agents to claim comment-only proof without inspecting the diff.
stop_conditions: The implementation needs installed repo-contract-kit behavior in the same packet.
Validation requires changing unrelated backlog rows or generated files outside the adapter output.

approval.notes: Active user goal is to implement remaining backlog items serially with one task agent per item.

token_budget.guidance: Use the listed files first; avoid broad repo rereads unless a validation failure requires it.

dependencies: AGW-078 completed adjacent ELI5/explainability prompt work.
Generated adapters must come from make prompt-adapters-export.
handoff.next_packet_hint: AGW-045 should handle latest-ADR discovery fallback and remain separate.
